decisionScope: Applicability disposition only. Operational status and liveClaim are separate evidence fields; no disposition by itself proves an integration is implemented or live.

sourcePins.oracleTasks: plan/gpt/oracle/tasks.md ORA-008, ORA-012, ORA-013, and ORA-015

entries.name: AgentCore Memory
entries.evidenceOrTrigger: The Oracle agent performs release-bound queries over immutable evidence. Reassess only if a persistent cross-turn conversational session becomes an approved product requirement.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: No conversational history, property-query memory, or evaluator identity state is copied into AgentCore Memory.
entries.releaseConsequence: No release dependency. Adding persistent conversational memory requires a new data-retention, authorization, deletion, and tenancy decision before implementation.

entries.name: AWS OIDC for GitHub Actions
entries.sourceContract: Team Kit integrate-ci-cd permissions and AWS credential stage
entries.owner: Oracle deployment owner with target AWS and repository administrators
entries.evidenceOrTrigger: Resolve when repository workflow-sharing authority, target-account trust policy, least-privilege role ownership, and a separately approved deployment path are verified, or when Ruslan approves the documented assessment exception.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: Validation-only pull-request CI receives no id-token permission, provider secret, or deployment authority. A later OIDC role must use short-lived credentials and an exact trust boundary.
entries.releaseConsequence: Blocks any claim of automated AWS deployment through shared CI/CD. Local validation may proceed; deployment remains a separate approval-gated operation.

entries.name: Team Kit frontend/backend architecture
entries.evidenceOrTrigger: ORA-010 and ORA-090 must prove shared TypeScript contracts, CDK, typed API boundaries, and deployment tests while using the approved private S3 plus CloudFront web host instead of the skill's Amplify example.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: One query and evidence contract shapes UI, API, MCP, and agent output; public and restricted fields remain separated at typed boundaries.
entries.releaseConsequence: Blocks the hosted product release until the adapted topology is implemented, synthesized, security-tested, and deep-link verified.

entries.name: Chat SDK and Asana adapter state
entries.sourceContract: Team Kit build-ai-agents Chat SDK and Asana guidance
entries.evidenceOrTrigger: The Oracle web, API, MCP, and named-tool agent path has no Asana-triggered chat surface. Reassess only if that surface is explicitly added.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: No property evidence, prompts, approvals, or evaluator state is copied into Chat SDK or Asana operational state.
entries.releaseConsequence: No release dependency for the approved Oracle scope. Introducing an Asana-triggered chat surface reopens architecture, access, data-handling, and test gates.

entries.name: Team Kit engineering guidelines
entries.evidenceOrTrigger: ORA-010 through ORA-015 must implement strict TypeScript service boundaries, CDK, tests, structured observability, and explicit exceptions for unavailable external operations integrations.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: Typed boundaries, secret redaction, public versus restricted data separation, and bounded telemetry are mandatory implementation controls.
entries.releaseConsequence: Blocks release until the applicable local controls pass. External Lexicon, dashboard, PagerDuty, and OIDC clauses remain governed by their separate pending entries.

entries.name: LangSmith telemetry
entries.sourceContract: Team Kit build-ai-agents prompt-iteration telemetry guidance
entries.owner: Oracle agent owner with privacy and telemetry account owners
entries.evidenceOrTrigger: Resolve only after package compatibility, account access, redaction, retention, regional handling, and approved property-data fields are verified, or after Ruslan approves the documented assessment exception.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: Prompts, named-tool inputs, citations, addresses, and restricted ownership evidence must not leave the approved boundary without field-level redaction and retention controls.
entries.releaseConsequence: Blocks calling prompt iteration fully Team Kit-conformant and blocks the full agent release until integration or an explicit exception is approved. Deterministic named queries remain unaffected.

entries.name: Lexicon metric registration
entries.owner: Oracle observability owner with the Lexicon repository owner
entries.evidenceOrTrigger: Resolve when change authority and the exact metric catalog destination are verified and registration evidence exists, or when Ruslan approves the documented assessment exception.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: Only metric definitions, units, dimensions, and ownership metadata may enter the catalog; no property row, prompt, credential, or personal identifier may be registered.
entries.releaseConsequence: Blocks unqualified Golden Path metric conformance and any metric-bearing production claim until the entry is resolved. No local stub counts as registration.

entries.name: Main Dashboard metric visualization
entries.owner: Oracle observability owner with the Main Dashboard repository owner
entries.evidenceOrTrigger: Resolve when dashboard change authority, target environment, metric identifiers, and widget verification are available, or when Ruslan approves the documented assessment exception.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: Dashboard widgets may contain aggregate operational metrics and release identifiers only; property details, prompts, credentials, and personal data are excluded.
entries.releaseConsequence: Blocks unqualified Golden Path dashboard conformance for emitted custom metrics. A screenshot, local JSON, or placeholder widget is not live-dashboard evidence.

entries.name: PagerDuty critical-failure alerting
entries.sourceContract: Team Kit observability-pagerduty-alerting and observability-dlq-alarms rules
entries.owner: Oracle operations owner with the PagerDuty service owner
entries.evidenceOrTrigger: Resolve when service ownership, production routing secret reference, target account, trigger and resolve tests, and on-call acknowledgement are verified, or when Ruslan approves the documented assessment exception.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: Alerts must contain redacted run, source, artifact, and failure-class identifiers only. Routing values live in an approved secret store and never in environment examples, logs, or evidence.
entries.releaseConsequence: Blocks a production claim for any critical workflow that can fail silently. Local fake endpoints or placeholder routing values do not satisfy the integration.

entries.name: Shared CI/CD workflows
entries.evidenceOrTrigger: ORA-012 and ORA-015 implement the exact six-recipe interface and a validation-only pull-request caller after workflow access is confirmed; deploy must fail closed outside a separately approved exact context.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: Pull-request CI receives read-only repository contents and no provider credentials, OIDC token, deployment context, publication key, or restricted dataset.
entries.releaseConsequence: Blocks release until local CI-equivalent checks and the least-permission validation caller pass. The adapted caller never treats a no-op deploy as successful conformance.
